primary_variables.matched_definition: Intervention using a compound that inhibits a target protein, pathway, or process.
evidence: incubated plasma and sterile acute wound fluids ex vivo with different concentrations of the endogenous proteases HNE and CG, or the bacterial proteases S. aureus V8 and P. aeruginosa LasB

attributes.dose_or_concentration: 3.33 μg per 1.00 mg of plasma (low); 33.3 μg per 1.00 mg of plasma (high)
attributes.route_or_context: ex vivo incubation of plasma and acute wound fluids
evidence: incubated plasma and sterile acute wound fluids ex vivo with different concentrations of the endogenous proteases HNE
For the endogenous proteases, these concentrations were 3.33 μg of HNE... per 1.00 mg of plasma
we also selected a ‘high’ condition, which was 10x higher amounts of each protease

attributes.dose_or_concentration: 5.55 milliunits per 1.00 mg of plasma (low); 55.5 milliunits per 1.00 mg of plasma (high)
attributes.route_or_context: ex vivo incubation of plasma and acute wound fluids
evidence: incubated plasma and sterile acute wound fluids ex vivo with different concentrations of the endogenous proteases... cathepsin G (CG)
For the endogenous proteases, these concentrations were... 5.55 milliunits of CG per 1.00 mg of plasma
we also selected a ‘high’ condition, which was 10x higher amounts of each protease

attributes.dose_or_concentration: 33.33 milliunits per 1.00 mg of plasma (low); 333.3 milliunits per 1.00 mg of plasma (high)
attributes.route_or_context: ex vivo incubation of plasma and acute wound fluids
evidence: incubated plasma and sterile acute wound fluids ex vivo with... the bacterial proteases... P. aeruginosa LasB
for the bacterial proteases, we used 33.33 milliunits of LasB... per 1.00 mg of plasma

attributes.dose_or_concentration: 0.17 units per 1.00 mg of plasma (low); 1.7 units per 1.00 mg of plasma (high)
attributes.route_or_context: ex vivo incubation of plasma and acute wound fluids
evidence: incubated plasma and sterile acute wound fluids ex vivo with... the bacterial proteases S. aureus V8
for the bacterial proteases, we used... 0.17 units of V8 per 1.00 mg of plasma
we also selected a ‘high’ condition, which was 10x higher amounts of each protease
Pooling the results into the five groups (undigested 24 h control, HNE, CG, LasB and V8)
clear differences between the different proteases, as well as between the low and high amounts of these enzymes

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment uses DD-MS2 (Data-Dependent Acquisition) and performs quantification via peptide counting/spectral counting, which aligns with label-free quantification (LFQ) logic.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The study explicitly describes a 'peptidomics approach' analyzing the 'low-molecular-weight peptidome' using 'nonspecific cleavage' settings in PEAKS, rather than standard tryptic digestion.
